summary_oneline: Publish AI-Parrot as an A2A connected agent in M365 Copilot with parrot-owned per-user tool credentials — most credential machinery already exists; new work is the A2A↔credential bridge.
summary: The brainstorm proposes building a per-user credential-acquisition system for an AI-Parrot agent exposed to M365 Copilot over A2A. Codebase research shows ~70% of the asserted 'must be built' machinery already exists: CredentialResolver (resolve→None→get_auth_url is exactly the link-out contract), a full OAuth2 IntegrationsService with a jira+o365 provider registry and state-nonce issuance, an OAuth callback web surface (oauth2_routes), the SuspendedExecutionStore (Redis suspend/resume), the AbstractTool output-scrubber seam, Entra OBO (o365.acquire_token_on_behalf_of), PermissionContext/PBAC, and VaultTokenSync. The AgentCard serialization fix has also already landed (camelCase + a documented decision to OMIT supportedInterfaces, superseding the brainstorm's dual-emit plan). The genuinely-new, in-scope work is therefore much narrower than the brainstorm implies: (1) the A2A↔credential bridge inside A2AServer.process_message (identity extraction + suspend-on-missing-credential + consent-link response + OAuth-callback-as-resume-trigger), (2) building the work-iq tool (which does not exist) on top of the existing OBO, (3) wiring fireflies as an MCP-credential integration (it is MCP-based, not a native toolkit), and (4) deciding the fate of the AuditLedger (which does not exist at all). Recommend /sdd-spec, but the spike gate (OQ#1 inbound identity) remains a hard empirical blocker that must be answered before the vault-keying design is committed.

localization.role: parrot-owned OAuth callback web surface, origin allowlist, handle_callback(code,state)
Redis suspend/resume store; resume trigger today is HITL message (new: OAuth callback)
camelCase card; deliberately omits supportedInterfaces (supersedes brainstorm §11 dual-emit)
GAP: no identity extraction, no credential check, no suspend path — the core new bridge
single output-scrubbing seam — 'no secrets in conversational plane' enforcement
Entra OBO implemented — reuse for work-iq + o365 cluster

constraints.claim: resolve()==None is the documented signal to surface get_auth_url() — the credential-acquisition flow is already a contract, not a new invention
constraints.implication: Build the A2A bridge ON this contract; do not invent a parallel resolver.

constraints.claim: OAuth callback route is excluded from auth middleware and validates return_origin against WEB_OAUTH_ALLOWED_ORIGINS
constraints.implication: A2A consent links must reuse this surface; new resume trigger must correlate callback→suspended A2A task by nonce.

constraints.claim: SuspendedExecutionStore resume today assumes a human-approver message on a parrot channel
constraints.implication: OAuth-callback / form-POST resume trigger is genuinely new, as the brainstorm states.

constraints.claim: AgentCard.to_dict already emits camelCase and deliberately omits supportedInterfaces (verified against a2a-dotnet)
constraints.implication: Brainstorm §11 dual-emit recommendation is superseded; remaining card risk is empirical autopopulate (OQ#9), not code.

constraints.claim: Output scrubbing happens at exactly one seam in AbstractTool
constraints.implication: Credential invariant #1/#3 has a real enforcement point to extend.

whats_new: A2A↔credential bridge in A2AServer.process_message: per-user identity derivation + suspend-on-missing-credential + TEXT consent-link response (F006).
OAuth-callback / form-POST resume trigger correlated by nonce to a suspended A2A task (F003+F004).
work-iq tool — does not exist anywhere; build on existing O365 OBO (F009, F010).
AuditLedger / key_fingerprint — does not exist; decide descope vs build (F007).
what_changes: fireflies wired as an MCP-credential integration reusing telegram mcp_persistence precedent, not a bespoke api-key form (F010).
untouched: OAuth2 IntegrationsService / provider registry / persistence schema (reuse as-is — F002).
integration_risks: Active in-flight 'a2a server + ms agent sdk' WIP (6d9b8b3ed) and FEAT-259 msagentsdk tenant-auth — coordinate to avoid divergence (F011).

OQ#1 identity is a hard blocker: if Copilot's low-code A2A connection carries no verifiable per-user identity, vault-keying needs a parrot-run Entra sign-in to bootstrap identity, changing the first-turn UX (F006).

confidence_map.claim: CredentialResolver exists with resolve/get_auth_url link-out contract
A full OAuth2 IntegrationsService + jira/o365 provider registry + state-nonce + persistence already exists
Parrot-owned OAuth callback web surface exists (oauth2_routes)
SuspendedExecutionStore exists; OAuth-callback resume trigger is new
confidence_map.reasoning: direct read; resume semantics documented as HITL-message

confidence_map.claim: AgentCard.to_dict already camelCase and intentionally omits supportedInterfaces — brainstorm §11 dual-emit superseded
confidence_map.reasoning: direct read incl. the documented decision NOTE

confidence_map.claim: A2AServer.process_message has no identity/credential/suspend hook — the core gap
AuditLedger / key_fingerprint do not exist anywhere
Entra OBO is implemented (o365.acquire_token_on_behalf_of) and reusable for work-iq
confidence_map.reasoning: grep + read of o365.py OBO methods

confidence_map.claim: work-iq does not exist; fireflies is MCP-based not a native toolkit
confidence_map.reasoning: zero work-iq hits; fireflies only under parrot/mcp + telegram MCP

confidence_map.claim: Copilot low-code A2A connection delivers a verifiable per-user identity to parrot
confidence_map.reasoning: spike gate OQ#1 confirmed YES by operator during Q&A; raises from low — still no codebase artifact to cite, hence medium not high

confidence_map.claim: work-iq public preview supports Entra OBO with a known resource id/scopes
confidence_map.reasoning: external API unknown (OQ#5); work-iq not in codebase

unknowns.question: [SPIKE GATE / OQ#1] Does Copilot Studio's low-code A2A connection deliver a stable, verifiable per-user identity to parrot in the request? If not, must parrot bootstrap identity via its own Entra sign-in on the first turn?
unknowns.user_answer: Yes — a verifiable per-user identity claim is present in the A2A request. Vault-keying can proceed directly; no parrot-run Entra sign-in needed to bootstrap identity. (The spec should still cite where in the payload the identity claim lands so process_message can extract it deterministically.)
plausible_answers: a) Yes — a verifiable identity claim is present in the A2A request
b) No — only chat history + locale + routing metadata; parrot must run its own Entra sign-in to establish identity
c) Partially — identity present only for Foundry-hosted agents, not the low-code connection

unknowns.question: How should the AuditLedger requirement (brainstorm §6 invariant #4 / §12 AC) be handled, given it does not exist in code at all?
unknowns.user_answer: Build the full append-only, KMS-signed AuditLedger as part of this feature. It is IN scope and greenfield (no existing code to extend); recording key_fingerprint per credentialed invocation is a hard acceptance criterion.
plausible_answers: a) Descope to a thin structured audit-log (no KMS signing) for v1
b) Build the full append-only KMS-signed AuditLedger as part of this feature
c) Defer audit entirely to a follow-up feature; out of scope for the A2A bridge

unknowns.question: Is fireflies' MCP server static-API-key or MCP-OAuth (OQ#6)? This decides whether to reuse telegram's mcp_persistence (vault_credential_name) as-is or add an MCP-OAuth authorization-code path.
unknowns.user_answer: Unknown — confirm during spec. Carry forward as an open spec question; bias toward static-API-key-over-MCP (reuse telegram mcp_persistence vault_credential_name) for v1 unless the MCP server is found to require OAuth.
plausible_answers: a) Static API key over MCP — reuse mcp_persistence vault_credential_name directly
b) MCP-OAuth — parrot becomes an MCP-OAuth client; new authorization-code surface
c) Both supported — pick static key for v1

unknowns.question: Does work-iq (Microsoft, public preview) actually support Entra OBO, and what are its resource id + required scopes + admin-consent path (OQ#5)? The tool itself must be built either way.
unknowns.user_answer: Unknown — spike/verify before building. The work-iq tool is greenfield regardless; verify OBO support + resource id + scopes empirically. If OBO is unavailable in preview, fall back to delegated auth-code 3LO via a new oauth2 provider (reusing the IntegrationsService registry pattern).
plausible_answers: a) Yes, OBO supported — build work-iq tool on o365.acquire_token_on_behalf_of with the documented scopes
b) No OBO in preview — fall back to delegated auth-code 3LO via a new oauth2 provider
c) Unknown until spiked — gate work-iq behind the identity spike

recommended_next_command.rationale: Localization and reuse story are high-confidence (C1-C9): the spec can be written against concrete, existing seams with a tightly-bounded new surface (the A2A↔credential bridge). Two hard gates must be encoded as spec preconditions, not silently assumed: the OQ#1 identity spike (U1) and the AuditLedger fate (U2). Because the new code is a bridge over verified primitives rather than an architectural fork, /sdd-brainstorm is not needed.

alternatives.when: If U1 cannot be answered from the existing spike logs — run the payload-inspection spike before committing vault-keying design.
Only if U1=b forces a parrot-run Entra sign-in bootstrap, which is an architectural fork worth exploring separately.
